subject: Cloud Computing
title: Google's Global Network
description: Learn Google's Global Network in one minute.

hook.text: Google owns one of the largest private networks on Earth — over 100,000 miles of fiber-optic cable.

buildup.text: This network connects Google's data centers at speeds most companies could never achieve.

discovery.text: GCP customers ride this network. Their traffic stays on Google's private fiber, avoiding public internet congestion.

twist.text: Google's network advantage is hard to replicate. Laying undersea cables costs hundreds of millions per line.

climax.text: Network performance is an invisible differentiator. Users don't see it, but their apps feel it.

punchline.text: Google's secret weapon is buried under the ocean.

quiz.question: How does Google's private network benefit GCP customers?
options: Traffic stays on Google's fast private fiber, avoiding public internet congestion
Customers get free internet access at home through Google's cables
The network encrypts data automatically without any customer configuration
